title: Polyalthia hirta
intro: Polyalthia hirta, also known as the Indian pepper tree, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it is used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Polyalthia hirta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Polyalthia hirta include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Polyalthia hirta is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples. The genus Polyalthia belongs to the tribe Polyalthieae, a group of plants known for their distinctive flowers and fruits.
